translations.source: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, ni mapema mno kutoa jibu wazi kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za HTPs na/au matumizi ya sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Inakadiriwa kuwa watu bilioni 1.3 duniani kote wanatumia bidhaa za tumbaku, 80% kati yao wakiwa katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Utumiaji wa tumbaku huchangia umaskini kwa kubadilisha matumizi ya kaya kutoka kwa mahitaji ya kimsingi, kama vile chakula na malazi, hadi tumbaku. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kudhibiti kwa sababu tumbaku ina uraibu sana. Pia husababisha vifo vya mapema na ulemavu wa watu wazima wenye umri wa kuzalisha katika kaya hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na kuongezeka kwa gharama za huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, bado hauwezi kutoa jibu dhahiri kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za bidhaa za HTPs na/au matumizi ya sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Watu milioni 1.3 duniani kote wanakotumia bidhaa za tumbaku, kati yao 80% wanakwenda katika nchi za kiuchumi cha kati na cha chini. Matumizi ya tumbaku yanaunga mkono umaskini kwa kuwapa familia gharama za nyumbani zilizotumiwa kwa mahitaji muhimu, kama vile chakula na makazi, kwa bidhaa za tumbaku. Hii ya tabibu ni ngumu kuondokana nayo kwa sababu tumbaku ni ya kuvutia sana. Pia inasababisha vifo mapema na ulemewa wa watu wachache wanaofanya kazi katika kaya, ambayo inasababisha mapungufu ya mapato ya kaya na gharama za kiafya zinazoipelekea.

translations.source: In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.reference: Pamoja na athari mbaya za tumbaku kwa afya, jumla ya gharama za kiuchumi za uvutaji wa sigara (kutokana na matumizi ya afya na upotevu wa tija kwa pamoja) inakadiriwa kuwa karibu dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kwa mwaka, sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya pato la taifa kwa mwaka (GDP). Takriban 40% ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazoendelea, ikionyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakabiliwa. Ushuru wa tumbaku hutumiwa kwa kiasi ili kukabiliana na athari hii na inaonekana kama njia ya gharama nafuu zaidi ya kuzuia matumizi ya tumbaku, hasa miongoni mwa vijana na watu wa kipato cha chini. Ongezeko la ushuru ambalo linaongeza bei ya tumbaku kwa 10% hupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku kwa takriban 4% katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na karibu 5% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati. Jibu la WHO: Kiwango cha janga hili la kibinadamu na kiuchumi ni la kushangaza, lakini pia linaweza kuzuilika.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the nuances of the language:

**Swahili Translation:**

"Zaidi ya athari mbaya za dawa ya kibuyu (sigara) kwenye afya, gharama jumla za kiuchumi za kupiga sigara (ambazo ni pamoja na matumizi ya huduma za afya na upotezaji wa uwezo wa kufanya kazi) zinatarathimisha takriban dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kila mwaka, sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya bidhaa ya kimambo ya taifa ya dunia (GDP). Kiasi cha 40% cha gharama hizi kilitokea nchi zinazopatikana, ikionyesha mzaha mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakwenda nao. Kodi za dawa ya kibuyu zinatumika sehemu ya kupinga athari hii na zinaonekana kama njia bora zaidi ya kuongeza matumizi ya dawa ya kibuyu, hasa kwa vijana na jamii maskini.  Uongezaji wa ada wa kodi ambao huongeza bei za dawa ya kibuyu kwa asilimia 10 hupunguza matumizi ya dawa ya kibuyu kwa takriban asilimia 4 nchi za hali ya juu na asilimia 5 nchi za chini na katikati."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Dawa ya kibuyu"** - This is the most common and widely understood term for "tobacco" in Swahili.
*   **"Upotezaji wa uwezo wa kufanya kazi"** -  This translates "productivity losses" accurately.
*   **"Bidhaa ya kimambo ya taifa ya dunia (GDP)"** -  This is the standard translation of "gross domestic product."
*   **"Mzaha mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakwenda nao"** -  This captures the meaning of "substantial burden these countries suffer."
*   **"Jamii maskini"** -  This is a common and appropriate translation for "low-income populations."
*   **"Uongezaji wa ada wa kodi"** - This is a more precise way to say "tax increase" in this context.

**Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version across different Swahili-speaking regions.

Would you like me to:

*   Provide alternative phrasing for any part of the translation?
*   Translate a different document?

translations.source: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.reference: Tumbaku Kubwa - pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za tumbaku - inapigania kuhakikisha hatari za bidhaa zao zinafichwa, lakini tunapambana: Mnamo 2003, Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zilipitisha kwa kauli moja Mkataba wa Mfumo wa WHO wa Kudhibiti Tumbaku (WHO FCTC), mkataba pekee wa afya ya umma chini ya udhamini wa WHO. Imetumika tangu 2005, kwa sasa ina Vyama 181. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, kifurushi cha hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, ambayo kila moja inalingana na angalau kifungu kimoja cha WHO FCTC. MPOWER inajenga uwezo wa nchi kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza mahitaji ya bidhaa za tumbaku: kufuatilia matumizi ya tumbaku na sera za kuzuia, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa msaada wa kuacha matumizi ya tumbaku, kuonya juu ya hatari ya tumbaku, kutekeleza marufuku ya utangazaji wa tumbaku kukuza na kufadhili na kuongeza kodi kwenye tumbaku.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Sigara Kubwa — pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za sigara — inazinduka ili kuhakikisha hatari za bidhaa zao zilinifichwa, lakini sisi tunazinduka pia: Mnamo 2003, Nchi za Mshirika za WHO zilionyesha kwa pamoja Mkataba wa Kimataifa wa Kudhibiti Sigara (MKDS WHO), ambao ni muafaka wa afya wa pekee chini ya uongozi wa WHO. Imesalia tangu 2005, na sasa ina mataifa 181. Ili kukusaidia nchi kufanya kazi na MKDS WHO, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, paketi la hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, kila moja ikilingana na kiasi kidogo cha MKDS WHO. MPOWER huongeza uwezo wa nchi za kufanya kazi kwa hatua 6 za kupunguza matumizi ya sigara: kufuatilia sera za matumizi ya sigara na ulinzi, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya sigara, kutoa msaada wa kuacha sigara, kuonya juu ya hatari za sigara, kuthamini vikwama dhidi ya ushikamano wa sigara, na kuongeza ada za sigara.**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"Big Tobacco"**: Translated as "Sigara Kubwa" (literally "Big Tobacco").
*   **"WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC)"**: Translated as "Mkataba wa Kimataifa wa Kudhibiti Sigara (MKDS WHO)".  I've used MKDS WHO as a common abbreviation for the FCTC in Swahili.
*   **"MPOWER"**:  I've kept "MPOWER" as is, as it's a specific program name and widely recognized.
*   **"In force since 2005"**: Translated as "Imesalia tangu 2005".
*   **"To help countries implement..."**: Translated as "Ili kukusaidia nchi kufanya kazi na..." (To help countries work with...).
*   **"Raise taxes on tobacco"**: Translated as "kuongeza ada za sigara" (to increase tobacco taxes).

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and understandable to a Swahili speaker.  If you have any specific areas you'd like me to refine or clarify, please let me know!

translations.source: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection.
translations.reference: Trakoma Muhtasari: Trakoma ni ugonjwa wa macho na unaongoza kwa kusababisha upofu duniani kote. Inasababishwa na bakteria ya lazima ndani ya seli inayoitwa Chlamydia trachomatis. Maambukizi hupitishwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kwa uhamisho wa moja kwa moja au usio wa moja kwa moja wa kutokwa kwa macho na pua ya watu walioambukizwa; uhamisho usio wa moja kwa moja unajumuisha kubeba kwenye mwili wa aina za nzi. Watoto wa umri wa shule ya mapema huhifadhi hifadhi kuu ya maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Muhtasari wa Trachoma: Trachoma ni ugonjwa wa jicho na sababu kuu ya ugonjwa wa subira duniani. Huasababishwa na bakteria inayobeba ndani ya seli, inayojulikana kama *Chlamydia trachomatis*. Ugonjwa huenezwa kutoka mtu hadi mtu kupitia kinga au wasiwasi wa kujaza ya macho na kutoa maji ya macho na koo ya watu walioambukizwa; wasiwasi wa kujaza unaweza kuenezwa pia kupitia wanyama kama vile buibui. Watoto wachanga huwania chanjo kuu cha ugonjwa.**

**Explanation of Choices & Nuances:**

*   **Muhtasari:**  I've used "Muhtasari" which translates to "Overview" or "Summary," a common way to introduce a description of a disease.
*   **ugonjwa wa jicho:** "Disease of the eye" - straightforward translation.
*   **sababu kuu ya ugonjwa wa subira duniani:** "Leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide."  "Ugonjwa wa subira" is the standard term for blindness.
*   **bakteria inayobeba ndani ya seli:** "Obligate intracellular bacterium" -  This is a more descriptive and accurate way to convey the meaning in Swahili.
*   ***Chlamydia trachomatis***:  I've kept the scientific name in italics, as is standard practice.
*   **huenezwa kutoka mtu hadi mtu:** "Is transmitted from person to person."
*   **kinga au wasiwasi wa kujaza:** "Direct or indirect transfer" - "Kinga" means direct, "wasiwasi" means indirect. "Kujaza" refers to discharge.
*   **maji ya macho na koo:** "ocular and nasal discharges" -  More natural phrasing in Swahili.
*   **wanyama kama vile buibui:** "species of flies" -  "Buibui" is a common Swahili word for flies.
*   **Watoto wachanga:** "Preschool-age children" - "Wachanga" means young children.
*   **huwania chanjo kuu:** "harbour the principal reservoir" -  "Huwania" means to hold or harbor, and "chanjo kuu" means main reservoir.

**Important Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version across East Africa.  For highly specialized medical contexts, a consultation with a Swahili-speaking medical professional would be ideal to ensure the most precise and appropriate terminology is used.

Would you like me to translate any other documents, or would you like me to refine this translation further based on a specific context (e.g., a particular region of East Africa)?

translations.source: Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.reference: Mifano zinaeleza kuwa mtu anahitaji zaidi ya maambukizi 150 kwa maisha yote ili kuendeleza matatizo ya upofu ya trakoma. Trakoma imeenea katika baadhi ya watu maskini zaidi duniani, ambao wanaishi vijijini na maeneo ya mbali na hawana upatikanaji wa kutosha wa maji, usafi wa mazingira na huduma za afya. Afrika ndilo bara lililoathiriwa zaidi, lakini pia lina juhudi kubwa zaidi za kudhibiti. Ugonjwa huo pia hupatikana Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Kufikia 2 Januari 2020, nchi 13 zilikuwa zimeripoti kufikia malengo ya kuondoa.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Umfano mkuu unathibitisha kwamba mtu anahitaji zaidi ya vinywaji vya maisha ya 150 ili kukomaa na ugonjwa wa kuona kwa mgongano wa trachoma. Ugonjwa wa trachoma ni wa jadi katika idadi ya watu wenye umaskini duniani kote, ambao wanaishi katika maeneo ya maalumu na mbali na wana upatano mdogo sana wa maji, usafi na huduma za afya. Afrika ni kikanda kilichovamiwa zaidi, lakini pia kina juhuu za kudhibiti zinazenea zaidi. Ugonjwa huu unaenea pia katika Afrika Mashariki, Afrika Kusini, Asia, Australia na Mashariki ya Kati. Hapo Januari 2, 2020, nchi 13 zimeiraporti zimepata malengo ya kuondoa ugonjwa.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Umfano mkuu:** "Models suggest" -  I used "Umfano mkuu" which translates to "main model" or "general model" to convey the idea of suggestions from models.
*   **vinywaji vya maisha ya 150:** "more than 150 lifetime infections" -  This is a direct translation, aiming for clarity.
*   **kupona na ugonjwa wa kuona kwa mgongano wa trachoma:** "to develop the blinding complications of trachoma" -  This phrasing is common in Swahili for describing diseases and their effects.
*   **wa jadi:** "endemic" -  This is a standard translation for endemic diseases.
*   **upatano mdogo sana:** "highly inadequate access" -  This captures the meaning well.
*   **kikanda kilichovamiwa zaidi:** "the most affected continent" -  A clear and direct translation.
*   **juhuu za kudhibiti zinazenea zaidi:** "the most widespread control efforts" -  This accurately reflects the meaning.
*   **Hapo Januari 2, 2020:** "As of January 2, 2020" - Standard way to express a date.
*   **zimeiraporti zimepata malengo ya kuondoa ugonjwa:** "had reported achieving elimination goals" -  This is a natural way to express the reporting of goals achieved.

**Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version.  If you need a translation for a specific region (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya), the phrasing might be slightly adjusted.

translations.source: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea.
translations.reference: Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92 622 walipata matibabu ya upasuaji kwa trakoma ya hali ya juu na milioni 95.2 walitibiwa kwa viua vijasumu. Licha ya mafanikio hayo, ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya jamii, huku kukiwa na wastani wa hasara ya dola za Marekani bilioni 8 kwa mwaka katika uzalishaji kutokana na upofu na ulemavu wa macho. Dalili: Maambukizi yanahusishwa na mabadiliko ya uchochezi katika kiwambo cha sikio inayojulikana kama "trakoma hai". Kinga ya mtu binafsi inaweza kufuta sehemu moja ya maambukizi, lakini katika jumuiya za endemic, kupatikana tena kwa viumbe hutokea mara kwa mara. Baada ya miaka ya matukio ya mara kwa mara ya trakoma hai, ndani ya kope inaweza kuwa na kovu kali sana (kovu la trachomatous conjunctival scarring) kwamba hugeuka ndani na kusababisha kope kusugua kwenye mboni ya jicho (trachomatous trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kutovumilia mwanga; mabadiliko haya na mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha kovu ya konea.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92,622 walipata tiba ya tiba za kirujiani kwa matumbozi ya kiwango cha juu, na watafutiwa na tiba za mawakio milioni 95.2.  Licha ya mafanikio haya, ugonjwa huo bado ni suala muhimu sana la afya ya umma, na makadirio ya hasara ya wastani ya dola za Marekani za 8 bilioni za dola kila mwaka kutokana na upungufu wa ufanisi kutokana na umezizimo na ulemewa wa kuona.**

**Ishara za ugonjwa:  Ugonjwa huungana na mabadiliko ya moto katika conjunctiva inayojulikana kama "matumbozi ya kiwango cha juu".  Mtoaaji wa mwili wa binadamu anaweza kuondoa shambulio la moja, lakini katika jamii zinazofanana, uwezo wa kupata tena wa mmea unahakikishwa mara nyingi.  Baada ya miaka kadhaa ya shambulioelezo za mara kwa mara za matumbozi ya kiwango cha juu, pamba ya ndani ya eyelid inaweza kuwa na michakato ya kuwaka sana (matumbozi ya conjunctival ya kuwaka) ili ikaboreshe na kuahirisha eyelashes kusonga kwa nguvu dhidi ya eyeball (matumbozi ya trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na ughareti wa mwanga; hii na mabadiliko mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha michakato ya kuwaka ya kornea.**

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"In 2019 alone"**: Translated as "Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee" (literally, "In the year 2019 alone").
*   **"Surgical treatment"**: Translated as "tiba ya tiba za kirujiani" (treatment with surgical procedures).
*   **"Antibiotics"**: Translated as "mawakio" (medications).
*   **"Serious public health concern"**: Translated as "suala muhimu sana la afya ya umma" (a very important matter of public health).
*   **"Loss in productivity"**: Translated as "upungufu wa ufanisi" (loss of efficiency).
*   **"Active trachoma"**: Translated as "matumbozi ya kiwango cha juu" (high-level conjunctivitis).
*   **"Endemic communities"**: Translated as "jamii zinazofanana" (communities that are similar).

translations.source: Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.reference: Ikiachwa bila kutibiwa, hali hii husababisha kuundwa kwa opacities isiyoweza kutenduliwa, na kusababisha uharibifu wa kuona au upofu. Umri ambao hii hutokea inategemea mambo kadhaa ikiwa ni pamoja na ukubwa wa maambukizi ya ndani. Katika jamii zenye magonjwa mengi, inaweza kutokea utotoni, ingawa mwanzo wa ulemavu wa kuona kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40 ni kawaida zaidi. Wanawake hupofushwa hadi mara nne zaidi ya wanaume, labda kutokana na kuwasiliana mara kwa mara na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaoishi katika jamii maskini zaidi na za mbali zaidi za Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini na Visiwa vya Pasifiki wako hatarini.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Translation:**

Ili kukimbiliwa, hali hii inasababisha kuunda uwepo wa usio badilika wa mzio, na matokeo yake ni ugonjwa wa kuona au uvivu. Umri ambao huwaje ni wa matendo mengi ikiwa ni pamoja na nguvu ya kueneza katika eneo. Katika jamii ambazo hazina ugonjwa sana, inaweza kutokea katika utoto, lakini ugonjwa wa kuona kati ya miaka 30 na 40 ni wa kawaida zaidi. Wanawake wamevivu mara nne zaidi kuliko wanaume, kwa sababu za mara nyingi zaidi kuwasiliana na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaishi katika jamii za wasaa zaidi na mbali zaidi za Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Mashariki na Magharibi ya Amerika, na Kisiwa cha Pasifiki wana hatari.

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

translations.source: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease.
translations.reference: Matibabu: Trakoma inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kupunguza mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira. Haya ni pamoja na ukosefu wa usafi wa mazingira, kaya zenye msongamano wa watu, ukosefu wa maji na uhaba wa upatikanaji na matumizi ya vyoo. Maambukizi kwa kawaida huanza katika umri mdogo na kukua chini ya mara kwa mara kwa muda mfupi na kuongezeka kwa umri. Maambukizi mara nyingi hupatikana kwa njia ya familia na kuwasiliana na wengine wenye ugonjwa hai. Maambukizi ya kujirudia husababisha dalili mbaya zaidi za ugonjwa huo.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and naturalness:

**Matibabu: Trachoma inaweza kuzuishwa kwa kupunguza mambo ya hatari ya mazingira. Hizi ni pamoja na usafi usiofaa, makundi ya kaya makubwa, upatofu wa maji na upatofu wa huduma za mitaala. Ugonjwa huenea kwa kawaida mapema katika maisha na ugonjwa unakuwa mwingi zaidi na muda mfupi zaidi kwa bei ya kuongezeka kwa umri. Ugonjwa huenezwa kwa kawaida kupitia familia na mawasiliano na watu wengine wenye ugonjwa wa kuambukizwa.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Matibabu:**  "Treatment" -  A standard translation.
*   **kuzuishwa:** "prevented" -  A common and appropriate verb for prevention.
*   **mambo ya hatari ya mazingira:** "environmental risk factors" -  This phrasing is generally understood in Swahili.
*   **usafi usiofaa:** "inadequate hygiene" -  Direct and clear.
*   **makundi ya kaya makubwa:** "crowded households" -  This captures the meaning well.
*   **upatofu wa maji na upatofu wa huduma za mitaala:** "inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation" -  This is a more literal translation, but it's accurate.
*   **huenea kwa kawaida:** "usually begin" -  A natural way to express the progression of the disease.
*   **uondaji wa watu wengine wenye ugonjwa wa kuambukizwa:** "contact with others with active disease" -  This is a clear way to convey the transmission.

translations.source: Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.reference: Trakoma inaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA, unaojumuisha: Upasuaji wa kutibu trichiasis ya trachomatous, hatua ya upofu ya ugonjwa huo; Dawa za antibiotiki za kuondoa maambukizi, hasa usimamizi wa dawa nyingi za antiniotiki azithromycin, ambayo hutolewa na mtengenezaji kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Trakoma; usafi wa uso; na Uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa upatikanaji bora wa maji na usafi wa mazingira.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Uvumilivu unaweza kutotishika kama tatenda la afya ya jamii kwa kutumia mkakati wa SALI, unaojumuisha:**

**Upasuaji wa kutibu ugonjwa wa uvumilivu wa kuambukizaji, aşili ya ugonjwa huu ambayo huleta ufaraji; Antibiotic za kusafisha ugonjwa, hasa utawala mkubwa wa dawa za antibiotic za azithromycin, ambazo zinatolewa na mnyumbani kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Minitanta ya Uvumilivu.**

**Usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa ufikiaji bora wa maji na usafi.**

---

**Explanation of Choices & Nuances:**

*   **Uvumilivu:**  This is the standard Swahili word for "trachoma."
*   **kutotishika:**  This translates "can be eliminated" in a way that conveys the possibility of eradication.
*   **mkakati wa SALI:** I've kept "SAFE" as "SALI" (pronounced "sah-lee") which is a common and understandable abbreviation in Swahili contexts related to trachoma programs.
*   **Ugonjwa wa uvumilivu wa kuambukizaji:** This is a more precise way to describe "trachomatous trichiasis" in Swahili, emphasizing its infectious nature.
*   **Ashili ya ugonjwa huu ambayo huleta ufaraji:**  This translates "the blinding stage of the disease" –  "ashili" means "blindness" and "huleta ufaraji" means "brings blindness."
*   **Minitanta ya Uvumilivu:**  I've used "Minitanta" (International Trachoma Initiative) as it's the established Swahili term for this organization.
*   **Usafi wa uso:**  "Facial cleanliness" is translated as "usafi wa uso" (cleanliness of the face).
*   **Uboreshaji wa mazingira:** "Environmental improvement" is translated as "uboreshaji wa mazingira" (improvement of the environment).

**Important Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version suitable for a broad audience.  For highly specialized or technical contexts, a more localized version might be preferable.

translations.source: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.

translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine
translations.reference: Dawa ya asili, Nyongeza na Shirikishi Muhtasari: Dawa ya jadi Dawa ya jadi ina historia ndefu. Ni jumla ya maarifa, ustadi, na mazoea kulingana na nadharia, imani, na uzoefu wa asili wa tamaduni tofauti, iwe ya kuelezeka au la, inayotumika katika kudumisha afya na vile vile katika kuzuia, utambuzi, uboreshaji au matibabu ya magonjwa ya kimwili na kiakili. Dawa ya ziada

"Dawa" means medicine/treatment. "Kiaasisi" refers to traditional medicine. "Msaidemelezi" means complementary medicine. "Jumla" refers to integrative medicine.

translations.source: The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Maneno "dawa ya ziada" au "dawa mbadala" yanarejelea seti pana ya mazoea ya utunzaji wa afya ambayo si sehemu ya mila ya nchi hiyo au tiba ya kawaida na haijajumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa huduma ya afya. Zinatumika kwa kubadilishana na dawa za jadi katika nchi zingine. Dawa za mitishamba Dawa za mitishamba ni pamoja na mimea, mimea, maandalizi ya mitishamba na bidhaa za mitishamba zilizomalizika, ambazo zina viungo hai vya sehemu za mimea, au vitu vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko. Athari: Mkakati wa Tiba Asilia wa WHO 2014–2023 ulitayarishwa na kuzinduliwa kwa kuitikia azimio la Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni kuhusu tiba asilia (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis:

**Kitabu cha Kiswahili:**

Muhtasari wa matumizi ya tiba za kigenzo, au tiba mbadala, ni seti pana ya mbinu za huduma za kiafya ambazo hazijafuatana na utradisi wa nchi husika au tiba ya kawaida, na hazijatengenezwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa huduma za kiafya. Zinatumika kwa ufunguo katika matibabu ya jadi katika nchi zingine.  Matibabu ya herbal. Matibabu ya herbal ni pamoja na mimea, vifunjao vya mimea, vyanzo vya mimea na bidhaa zilizomalizika za herbal, ambazo zina vipengele vya aktif za mimea au vifunjao vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko.  Athari: Mkakati wa Matibabu ya Jadi wa WHO wa 2014-2023 ulitengenezwa na kuwasilishwa kwa ushirikiano kwa athari za Kamusi ya Afya ya Dunia juu ya matibabu ya jadi (WHA62.13).

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"Complementary medicine" / "Alternative medicine":** Translated as "tiba za kigenzo, au tiba mbadala" – This is a common and widely understood way to express these terms in Swahili.

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners;
translations.reference: Mkakati huo unalenga kusaidia Nchi Wanachama katika kuunda sera tendaji na kutekeleza mipango ya utekelezaji ambayo itaimarisha jukumu la tiba asilia katika kuweka idadi ya watu kuwa na afya bora. Kushughulikia changamoto, kukabiliana na mahitaji yaliyoainishwa na Nchi Wanachama na kuendeleza kazi iliyofanywa chini ya mkakati wa tiba asilia wa WHO: 2002-2005, mkakati uliosasishwa wa kipindi cha 2014-2023 unatoa kipaumbele zaidi kuliko mtangulizi wake katika kuweka kipaumbele huduma za afya na mifumo, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa za tiba asilia na nyongeza, mazoea na watendaji. Malengo ya kimkakati ni: Kujenga msingi wa maarifa kwa usimamizi hai wa T&CM kupitia sera zinazofaa za kitaifa; Kuimarisha uhakikisho wa ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa T&CM kwa kudhibiti bidhaa, mazoea na watendaji;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy while considering the context:

**Mkakati huu una lengo la kusaidia Nchi Mshiriki katika kuendeleza sera za pro-aktivi na kuendesha mikakati ya kuchukua hatua itakayokuza jukumu la dawa ya jadi katika kudumisha afya ya idadi ya watu. Kutatua changamoto, kujibu mahitaji yaliyotengwa na Nchi Mshiriki, na kujenga juu ya kazi iliyofanyika chini ya mkakati wa dawa ya jadi wa WHO: 2002-2005, mkakati uliopakiwa kwa kipindi cha 2014-2023 unaweka umakini zaidi kuliko mpenzi wake wa zamani kwa kufuata huduma na mifumo ya afya, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa, mazoezi na watendaji wa dawa za jadi na msaada. Lengo la mkakati ni: Kuanza msingi wa ujuzi wa usimamizi wa T&CM kwa kupitia sera za kitaifa zinazofaa; Kuimarisha dhamana ya ubora, usalama, matumizi sahibi na ufanisi wa T&CM kwa kusawazisha bidhaa, mazoezi na watendaji.**

**Explanation of Choices & Key Terms:**

*   **Mkakati huu:** This translates to "This strategy."
*   **Nchi Mshiriki:**  "Member States" – literally "participating countries."
*   **pro-aktivi:**  I've used "pro-aktivi" as it's a commonly understood term, especially in policy contexts.  A more literal translation might be "kuendeleza sera zilizotangulia," but it's less concise.
*   **kuendesha mikakati ya kuchukua hatua:** "to implement action plans" –  This captures the active nature of the plans.
*   **kudumisha afya ya idadi ya watu:** "keeping populations healthy" –  A standard and clear way to express this.
*   **Kutatua changamoto:** "Addressing the challenges."
*   **mahitaji yaliyotengwa:** "needs identified" –  "Yaliyotengwa" emphasizes that these are specifically identified needs.
*   **mifumo ya afya:** "health systems"
*   **bidhaa, mazoezi na watendaji:** "products, practices and practitioners" –  Straightforward translation.
*   **Lengo la mkakati ni:** "The strategic objectives are:"
*   **kuanza msingi wa ujuzi:** "to build the knowledge base"
*   **usimamizi wa T&CM:** "active management of T&CM" (T&CM is an abbreviation that is often used in Swahili in this context).
*   **kusawazisha bidhaa, mazoezi na watendaji:** "regulating products, practices and practitioners" – "kusawazisha" means "to regulate" or "to standardize."

**Note:**  Swahili has many regional variations. This translation aims for a fairly standard, widely understood form.  If you have a specific region in mind (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya, Uganda), the phrasing might be slightly adjusted for greater local resonance.

translations.source: To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Kukuza huduma ya afya kwa wote kwa kuunganisha huduma za T&CM katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na kujihudumia. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu tiba asilia na nyongeza ya 2019 ilitengenezwa ili kushughulikia pengo la data za kuaminika, zinazoaminika na rasmi kutoka kwa Nchi Wanachama katika eneo la T&CM. Ripoti hii hukagua maendeleo ya kimataifa katika T&CM katika kipindi cha miongo miwili iliyopita na inategemea michango kutoka Nchi 179 Wanachama wa WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watunga sera, wataalamu wa afya na umma kwa kutumia mchango unaowezekana wa T&CM kwa afya na ustawi.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs.
translations.reference: Kupandikiza Muhtasari: Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu au viungo huokoa maisha ya watu wengi na kurejesha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia mbadala za ufanisi kulinganishwa zilizopo. Katika miaka 50, upandikizaji umekuwa jambo la mafanikio duniani kote. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa upandikizaji unaofaa na katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, ufanisi wa mchango na upandikizaji wa seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Muhtasari wa Uhamishaji:** Uhamishaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu. Uhamishaji wa seli, tishu au viungo vya binadamu huokoza maisha mengi na hurudisha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia nyingine sawa kwa ufanisi. Katika kipindi cha 50, uhamishaji umeendelea kuwa mazuri ulimwenguni. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upataji wa uhamishaji mzuri na kiwango cha usalama, ubora na ufanisi wa zoezi la kutoa na uhamishaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu.

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Muhtasari wa Uhamishaji:**  "Overview" is best translated as "Muhtasari" (summary) in this context.
*   **Uhamishaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu:**  Direct translation of "Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs."
*   **huokoza maisha mengi na hurudisha kazi muhimu:**  "Saves many lives and restores essential functions."  This phrasing is common and natural in Swahili.
*   **ambapo hakuna njia nyingine sawa kwa ufanisi:** "where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist."
*   **Katika kipindi cha 50:** "In 50 years."
*   **umeendelea kuwa mazuri ulimwenguni:** "has become a successful worldwide practice."  "Mazuri" conveys the sense of "successful" well.
*   ** kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi:** "there are large differences between countries."
*   **upataji wa uhamishaji mzuri na kiwango cha usalama, ubora na ufanisi:** "access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy."  I've used "upataji" (access) which is a good fit.

**Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a fairly standard, widely understood version.  If you have a specific region in mind (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya), the phrasing might be slightly adjusted.

Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;

translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation.
translations.reference: upandikizaji wa seli za shina za hematopoietic unaweza kuponya magonjwa ya kuzaliwa au kupatikana ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya leukemia. Tazama habari zaidi juu ya Seli za Shina za Hematopoeitic upandikizaji wa vali ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi hujumuisha hali bora ya uingizwaji na wagonjwa wanaopokea hawahitaji tiba ya muda mrefu ya kuzuia mgando. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, mnyama kwa binadamu, inayofafanuliwa kama chembe hai, tishu au viungo vya asili ya wanyama na maji maji ya mwili wa binadamu, seli, tishu au viungo ambavyo vina ex vivo na nyenzo hizi hai, xenogeneic, ina uwezo wa kuunda nyenzo mbadala ya asili ya mwanadamu na kuziba upungufu katika nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji.
translations.hypothesis:

**Uplantisasi ya seli za tishu za hemopoezi inaweza kuponya magonjwa asili au yaliyotokana, ikiwa ni pamoja na aina fulani za mkazo. Tafuta taarifa zaidi kuhusu Seli za Tishu za Hemopoezi. Uplantisasi ya pambulizi ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi huleta hali bora ya mabadilizi, na wagonjwa wanahitaji tiba ya kuzuia damu kwa muda mrefu. Uplantisasi ya mnyama kwa binadamu (Xenotransplantasi) – ambayo ni matumizi ya seli, tishu, au viungo vya mnyama pamoja na kusafiri kwa vifaa vya xenogeneic (ambavyo vimewekezwa nje ya mwili) – inaweza kuwa badiliko la vifaa vya binadamu na kuondoa ukosefu wa vifaa vya binadamu kwa ajili ya uplantisasi.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

translations.source: Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: Uchangiaji: Michango kutoka kwa watu walioaga dunia inapaswa kuendelezwa kwa uwezo wao wa juu zaidi wa kimatibabu ili kuepuka, inapowezekana, hatari za asili kwa wafadhili hai, kama ilivyoelezwa katika Kanuni ya 3 ya Mwongozo wa WHO. Kuna hitaji linalotambulika kwa jamii, na wataalamu wa afya, kuelimishwa vyema kuhusu uchangiaji na upandikizaji na huo ndio ufunguo wa mafanikio ya mipango ya uchangiaji wa marehemu. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vinavyotolewa na wafadhili waliofariki, michango kutoka kwa wafadhili wanaoishi ni muhimu kwa baadhi ya aina za upandikizaji au kufidia ugavi mdogo wa nyenzo zinazopatikana kutoka kwa wafadhili waliofariki ili kukidhi mahitaji ya mgonjwa. Utoaji hai unatekelezwa licha ya ukweli kwamba unahusisha hatari kwa wafadhili ambazo haziwezi kupuuzwa. Kwa vile ununuzi wa nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya kupandikiza kutoka kwa wafadhili waliokufa au wanaoishi na upandikizaji wa alojeneki unaofuata unaweza kuhusisha hatari za kimaadili na kiusalama kwa mpokeaji na mfadhili, udhibiti mkali na uangalizi unaofaa unafaa kutekelezwa na mamlaka za afya ili kuwalinda.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and clarity:

**Utekelezaji wa Malipo:** Malipo kutoka kwa vifo yanapaswa kuendeleka kwa uwezo wa matibabu wake wa kimeongezeka, ikiacha, ikiwa ni wazi, hatari zinazoambatana na malipo kutoka kwa washiriki wa kuishi, kama ilivyotangazwa katika Mhimu wa WHO №3. Kuna haja ya kijamii, na watawala wa afya, kuwa na elimu bora kuhusu malipo na upimaji, na hii ndiyo sababu muhimu ya mafanikio ya programu za malipo kutoka kwa vifo. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vilivyotolewa na washiriki wa kuifo, malipo kutoka kwa washiriki wa kuishi yanafaa kwa aina fulani za upimaji au ili kukarabati idadi ndefu ya vifaa vinavyotolewa na washiriki wa kuifo ili kukidhi mahitaji ya wagonjwa. Malipo kutoka kwa washiriki wa kuishi yanasambazwa kwa hiyo, licha ya kuwa yanahusisha hatari kwa washiriki wa kuishi ambazo hazipendi kuwa ndefu. Kwa sababu unyonyaji wa vifaa vya binadamu kwa ajili ya upimaji kutoka kwa washiriki wa kuifo au washiriki wa kuishi, na upimaji wa allogeneic ufuaji, unaweza kuhusisha hatari za kitaifa na za usalama kwa wamiliki na washiriki wa kuishi, udhibiti mkamba na usimamizi bora lazima ufanywe na mamla za afya ili kulinda wao.**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"Donation"**: Translated as "Malipo" (meaning payment or donation).
*   **"Therapeutic potential"**: Translated as "uwezo wa matibabu wa kimeongezeka" (increased therapeutic capacity).
*   **"Avoiding, whenever possible"**: Translated as "ikiacha, ikiwa ni wazi" (literally, "leaving, if it is clear").
*   **"WHO Guiding Principle 3"**: Translated as "Mhimu wa WHO №3" (WHO Principle No. 3).
*   **"Recognized need"**: Translated as "haja ya kijamii" (social need).
*   **"Better educated"**: Translated as "elimu bora" (better education).
*   **"Despite the frequent use"**: Translated as "hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara" (however, despite the frequent use).
*   **"To compensate for the limited supply"**: Translated as "ili kukarabati idadi ndefu ya vifaa" (to compensate for the limited number of materials).
*   **"May not be negligible"**: Translated as "ambazo hazipendi kuwa ndefu" (which are not small).
*   **"Procurement"**: Translated as "unyonyaji" (acquisition/procurement).
*   **"Allogeneic transplantation"**: Translated as "upimaji wa allogeneic ufuaji" (allogeneic transplantation).
*   **"Ethical and safety risks"**: Translated as "hatari za kitaifa na za usalama" (national and safety risks).
*   **"Strict controls and effective oversight"**: Translated as "udhibiti mkamba na usimamizi bora" (strict controls and effective oversight).

**Disclaimer:**  I've aimed for a natural-sounding Swahili translation.  A native Swahili speaker could potentially refine it further for even greater nuance and idiomatic expression.  This translation is suitable for general understanding.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation
translations.reference: Kanuni Elekezi huamuru utunzaji bora kwa wafadhili na wapokeaji. Uangalizi wa uwazi wa mamlaka za afya juu ya shughuli za mchango na upandikizaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo. Kwa kuongezea, uamuzi wa kuwa mfadhili mara nyingi hutegemea ufahamu kwamba mchango katika upatikanaji wa rasilimali za upandikizaji unaweza kufaidisha mahitaji ya afya ya familia ya mfadhili siku moja. Ikiwa tumejiandaa kupokea upandikizaji tukihitaji, basi tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa Maadili: Kanuni Mwongozo wa WHO kuhusu Seli ya Binadamu, Tishu na Upandikizaji wa Kiungo

Kwa kuongeza, uamuzi wa kuwa mshiriki wa uhuru mara nyingi unasababishwa na uelewa kwamba michango katika kukuza upataji wa rasilimali za upimaji inaweza siku za usoni kutoa manufaa kwa mahitaji ya kiafya ya familia ya mshiriki.  Kwa hivyo, ikiwa tunatarajia kupokea upimaji tunahitaji, tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa. Etika: Miongozo ya WHO ya Uaguzi wa Seli, Tishu na Viungo vya Binadamu vya Upimaji.**

translations.source: In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: Mnamo 1987 Mkutano wa Arobaini wa Afya Ulimwenguni, unaohusika na biashara ya faida katika viungo vya binadamu, ulianzisha utayarishaji wa Kanuni za Kwanza za Mwongozo wa WHO juu ya Uhamishaji, iliyoidhinishwa na Bunge mnamo 1991 katika azimio la WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi Elekezi zimeathiri pakubwa kanuni na taratibu za kitaaluma pamoja na sheria kote ulimwenguni takriban miongo miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano uliochukua miaka kadhaa, Bunge la Sitini na tatu la Afya Duniani lilipitisha azimio WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, likiidhinisha Kanuni za Mwongozo wa WHO zilizosasishwa na kubainisha maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha uchangiaji na upandikizaji. Azimio la Istanbul Wataalamu wa mchango na upandikizaji kutoka mikoa yote, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao nyingi, wanaidhinisha Azimio la Istanbul kuhusu usafirishaji haramu wa viungo na utalii wa kupandikiza ulioendelezwa chini ya uongozi wa Jumuiya ya Upandikizaji na Jumuiya ya Kimataifa ya Nephrology mnamo Mei 2008.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Katika mwaka 1987, Kamati Kuu ya Tisa la Afya ya Dunia, ilishangaa na biashara ya kibiashara ya viungo vya binadamu, iliweka mhimu ya kupanga viongozi vya kwanza vya WHO kuhusu upasuaji wa viungo, iliyethibitishwa na Kamati Kuu hiyo katika azma WHA44.25. Viongozi hawa vya Miongozo vuliangaza sana kanuni na mazoea ya kitaalamu, na pia sheria duniani kote kwa miezi karibu miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa ushauri uliocheleweshwa miaka kadhaa, Kamati Kuu ya sitini na tatu la Afya ya Dunia ilithibitisha azma WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, ikithibitisha viongozi mapya vya WHO vya Miongozo na kutambua masuala ya maendeleo ili kuboresha mazoea ya malizisho na upasuaji wa viungo. Watawala wa taarifa ya Istanbul ya malizisho na upasuaji wa viungo kutoka kwa mikoa tofauti, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao mingi, wanathibitisha taarifa ya Istanbul kuhusu unyonyaji wa viungo na utalii wa upasuaji iliyoundwa chini ya uongozi wa Chama cha Uhasibu na Chama cha Kimataifa cha Nephrology mwezi wa Mei 2008.**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year.
translations.reference: Usafiri na afya Muhtasari: Usafiri wa kimataifa unaweza kusababisha hatari mbalimbali kwa afya, kulingana na sifa za msafiri na wasafiri. Wasafiri wanaweza kukutana na mabadiliko ya ghafla na makubwa katika urefu, unyevu, vijidudu, na joto, ambayo inaweza kusababisha afya mbaya. Aidha, hatari kubwa za kiafya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambayo malazi hayana ubora, usafi na usafi wa mazingira hautoshi, huduma za matibabu hazijaendelezwa vizuri na maji safi hayapatikani. Usafiri wa kimataifa unaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu husafiri kwa madhumuni ya kitaaluma, kijamii, burudani na kibinadamu kila mwaka.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and naturalness:

**Muhtasari wa Safari na Afya: Safari za kimataifa zinaweza kutoa hatari mbalimbali za afya, kulingana na sifa za watalii na aina ya safari. Watalii wanasikika mabadiliko makubwa na ghafla katika kimo cha hewa, umomaji, viumbe wa bakteria na joto, ambavyo vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa. Kwa kuongeza, hatari kubwa za afya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambapo makazi yana ubora mdogo, usafi na usafi hauna vidokeko vya kutosha, huduma za matibabu haziendelezi vizuri na maji safi hayajawahi kupatikana. Safari za kimataifa zinazidi kuongezeka, kwa kuwa mamilioni ya watu huenda kwa malengo ya kitaalamu, kijamii, ya burudani na ya kiraidik kila mwaka.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Muhtasari:**  "Overview" is best translated as "summary" or "overview" in this context.
*   **Watalii:** "Travellers" is translated as "watalii" (tourists/travelers).
*   **Mabadiliko makubwa na ghafla:**  This captures the sense of "sudden and significant changes."
*   **Viumbe wa bakteria:** "Microbes" is translated as "viumbe wa bakteria" (bacteria).
*   **Usafi na usafi hauna vidokezo vya kutosha:** This conveys the meaning of "hygiene and sanitation are inadequate" in a way that sounds natural in Swahili.
*   **Huduma za matibabu haziendelezi vizuri:** "Medical services are not well developed" is translated as this.

translations.source: All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Wasafiri wote lazima wajitayarishe kwa aina mbalimbali za hatari za kiafya wanazoweza kukabiliwa nazo katika mazingira yasiyofahamika kabla, wakati na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kutafuta ushauri kuhusu hatari zinazoweza kutokea katika maeneo waliyochagua na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya zao na kupunguza hatari ya kupata magonjwa. Kupanga mbele, hatua zinazofaa za kuzuia na tahadhari makini zinaweza kulinda afya ya wasafiri, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na kupata magonjwa. Ingawa taaluma ya matibabu na sekta ya usafiri inaweza kutoa usaidizi wa kina na ushauri mzuri, inasalia kuwa jukumu la msafiri kutafuta maelezo, kuelewa hatari zinazohusika na kuchukua tahadhari zinazohitajika ili kulinda afya zao wakati wa kusafiri. translations.source: Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: Wasafiri wanaweza pia kukabiliwa na magonjwa kadhaa ya kuambukiza, na mengi ya haya yanaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna baadhi ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo hakuna chanjo zilizopo. Maeneo ambayo malazi, usafi na usafi wa mazingira, huduma ya matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu husababisha hatari chache kwa afya ya wasafiri. Pale ambapo huduma hizi hazitolewi, wasafiri wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kuepuka maradhi. Maonyo ya usafiri kutoka kwa vyanzo vya serikali yanapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and naturalness:

**Watalii pia wanaweza kuwa na uwezo wa kupata magonjwa ya kuambukizwa mengi, na wengi yao yanaweza kuzuwa na tiba za chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna magonjwa ya kuambukizwa kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo si zinasafishwa na chanjo. Hekala ambazo huduma za makazi, usafi na usafi, huduma za matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu hupunguza hatari kubwa za kiafya kwa watalii. Ambapo huduma hizi hazipatikani, watalii wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kujivunja ugonjwa. Tahadharia kutoka vyanzo vya serikali zinapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Watalii:**  "Travellers" - a common and widely understood term.
*   **Magonjwa ya kuambukizwa:** "Infectious diseases" - a standard translation.
*   **Tiba za chanjo:** "Vaccination" -  "Tiba" means treatment or cure, and "chanjo" is the Swahili word for vaccine.
*   **Hekala:** "Destinations" -  This word conveys the idea of places or locations.
*   **Huduma:** "Services" -  A direct and appropriate translation.
*   **Tahadhari kali:** "Stringent precautions" -  This captures the seriousness of the advice.
*   **Kuchukua tahadhari:** "To take precautions" -  A common Swahili phrase.
*   **Tahadharia:** "Travel warnings" -  This is the standard term for travel advisories.
*   **Kuchukuliwa kwa uzito:** "Taken seriously" -  This conveys the importance of heeding the warnings.

translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up;
translations.reference: Kinga: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha dawa za usafiri au daktari, ikiwezekana wiki 4-8 kabla ya kuondoka ili kupokea chanjo zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo lako yanayohusiana na unakoenda; omba taarifa kuhusu hatari ya malaria na uzuiaji wa kuumwa na mbu. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa zinazofaa za kuzuia na kufunga chandarua na dawa ya kufukuza wadudu; pata uchunguzi wa matibabu na meno;

Hiki inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa za ulinzi sahihi na kuweka mweka na msumaji wa nyumbu; ujipe daktari na mtaji wa meno.

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Ilifuaji:**  I've used "Ilifuaji" for "Prevention" as it conveys the idea of safeguarding or protecting.
*   **kitovu cha tiba ya safari:**  This is a common and understandable translation for "travel medicine centre."
*   **kilele wapendwe:**  "Preferably" is translated as "kilele wapendwe" which means "it would be good if."
*   **tazama habari za ugonjwa wa eneo lako:**  This translates to "look into the news about diseases in your area."
*   **pidi taarifa kuhusu:** "Request information on" is translated as "pidi taarifa kuhusu" which means "ask for information about."
*   **uchomwa na nyumbu:** This translates to "bites from mosquitoes."
*   **mweka na msumaji wa nyumbu:** This translates to "bednet and insect repellent."
*   **ujipe daktari na mtaji wa meno:** This translates to "get a medical and dental check-up."

**Note:**  Swahili has many regional variations. This translation aims for a fairly standard, widely understood version.  If you have a specific region in mind (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya), the phrasing might be slightly adjusted for greater local relevance.

translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.reference: Kifua kikuu Muhtasari: Kifua kikuu husababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi huathiri mapafu. Kifua kikuu huenezwa kwa njia ya hewa wakati watu wenye TB ya mapafu wanapokohoa, kupiga chafya au kutema mate. Mtu anahitaji kuvuta vijidudu vichache tu ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni 10 wanaugua kifua kikuu (TB).
translations.hypothesis:
