TB huenezwa hewani wakati watu walio na ugonjwa wa mapafu wanapokuchwa, kunyunyiza, au kuongea. Mtu anahitaji kuinua tu baadhi ya vimelea ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni kumi wanakufa na TB.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Muhtasari wa Tukufu:**  This translates to "Overview" or "Summary" and is a common and respectful way to introduce a topic in Swahili.
*   **vimelea:** This is the standard Swahili word for "bacteria."
*   **husababisha ugonjwa wa mapafu:** This translates to "causes lung disease."
*   **huenezwa hewani:** This means "spread through the air."
*   **kuinua:** This translates to "inhale" or "breathe in."
*   **kuambukizwa:** This means "to be infected."

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and easily understandable for a Swahili speaker.  If you have any specific nuances you'd like me to consider, or if you'd like me to adjust the translation for a particular context, please let me know!

translations.source: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Licha ya kuwa ni ugonjwa unaozuilika na unaotibika, watu milioni 1.5 hufa kutokana na TB kila mwaka - na kuifanya kuwa muuaji mkuu wa kuambukiza duniani. Kifua kikuu ndicho kisababishi kikuu cha vifo vya watu walio na VVU na pia mchangiaji mkubwa katika ukinzani wa viua viini Watu wengi wanaougua TB wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, lakini TB ipo duniani kote. Takriban nusu ya watu wote walio na TB wanaweza kupatikana katika nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Ufilipino na Afrika Kusini. Takriban robo ya idadi ya watu duniani inakadiriwa kuwa wameambukizwa bakteria wa TB, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea kupata ugonjwa wa TB na wengine wataondoa maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis: Hata licha ya kuwa ni ugonjwa unaogopwa na unaorudishwa, watu milioni 1.5 huufa kila mwaka kutokana na kuvimbiya – ikifanya iwe wasichaji wa vifo vya kuambukiza zaidi duniani. Kuvimbiya ndio sababu kuu ya vifo vya watu walio na ugonjwa wa AIDS na pia ni mchangamfu mkubwa wa usuliaji wa bakteria. Watu wengi waliopata ugonjwa wa kuvimbiya wanaishi nchi za kipato cha chini na katikati, lakini kuvimbiya inapatikana duniani kote. Watu takribani nusu wote walio na kuvimbiya wanaweza kupatikana nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines na Afrika Kusini. Watu takribani robo moja ya idadi ya watu duniani wamejiathiri na bakteria za kuvimbiya, lakini watu wengi hawatazidi kuunda ugonjwa wa kuvimbiya na wengine wataondoa ugonjwa huo.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.reference: Wale ambao wameambukizwa lakini sio (bado) wagonjwa na ugonjwa huo hawawezi kuusambaza. Watu walioambukizwa na bakteria wa TB wana hatari ya maisha ya 5-10% ya kuugua TB. Wale walio na kinga dhaifu, kama vile watu wanaoishi na VVU, utapiamlo au kisukari, au watu wanaotumia tumbaku, wana hatari kubwa ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa TB ni pamoja na: Kikohozi cha muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, udhaifu au uchovu, kupungua uzito, homa, jasho la usiku. Mara nyingi, dalili hizi zitakuwa nyepesi kwa miezi mingi, hivyo kusababisha kuchelewa kutafuta huduma na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza maambukizi kwa wengine.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow:

**Waote walioathirika lakini bado hawana ugonjwa, wasiowezi kueneza ugonjwa huo. Watu walioathirika na bakteria za kuvimba ya kifua kikuu (TB) wana uwezo wa 5-10% wa kuugua kuvimba hicho kwa maisha yao. Watu wenye mifugo dhaifu, kama vile wale walio na ugonjwa wa HIV, ukosefu wa afya, ugonjwa wa kusafisha damu au ugonjwa wa mmea, wana uwezo wa juu wa kuugua. Ishara za ugonjwa wa kuvimba hicho ni pamoja na:**

**Kucheka muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, nguvu za chini au uchovu, kupungua kwa uzito, joto, na kulala na joto. Kwa mara nyingi, ishara hizi itakuwa hafifu kwa miezi mingi, ikitoa fursa za kutua katika utafuti wa huduma za kiafya na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza ugonjwa kwa wengine.**

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"Those who are infected but not (yet) ill..."**  Translated as "Waote walioathirika lakini bado hawana ugonjwa..." (All those infected but still not sick...).  I've aimed for a clear and direct rendering.
*   **"TB bacteria"** Translated as "bakteria za kuvimba ya kifua kikuu (TB)" -  This is the standard Swahili term for TB bacteria.  I've included the abbreviation in parentheses for clarity.
*   **"Compromised immune systems"** Translated as "mifugo dhaifu" (weak immune systems).
*   **"Malnutrition"** Translated as "ukosefu wa afya" (lack of health/nutrition).
*   **"People who use tobacco"** Translated as "wale walio na mmea" (those who use tobacco).
*   **"Symptoms"** Translated as "Ishara za ugonjwa" (Signs of the disease).
*   **"Prolonged cough"** Translated as "Kucheka muda mrefu" (coughing for a long time).
*   **"Chest pain"** Translated as "maumivu ya kifua" (pain in the chest).
*   **"Weakness or fatigue"** Translated as "nguvu za chini au uchovu" (low strength or fatigue).
*   **"Weight loss"** Translated as "kupungua kwa uzito" (loss of weight).
*   **"Fever"** Translated as "joto" (heat/fever).
*   **"Night sweats"** Translated as "kulala na joto" (sleeping with heat).
*   **"Delays in seeking care"** Translated as "kutua katika utafuti wa huduma za kiafya" (delaying in seeking medical services).
*   **"Spreading the infection"** Translated as "kueneza ugonjwa" (spreading the disease).

I've tried to use common and easily understood Swahili vocabulary.  If you have any specific context or desired nuances, please let me know, and I can refine the translation further.

translations.source: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Mtoa huduma wa afya akishuku mgonjwa kuwa na ugonjwa wa TB, atampeleka mgonjwa kupima. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa unaoshukiwa wa TB ya mapafu, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya makohozi kwa ajili ya kupima bakteria wa TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa mapafu, sampuli za maji na tishu zilizoathirika za mwili zinaweza kupimwa. WHO inapendekeza vipimo vya haraka vya uchunguzi wa molekuli kama vipimo vya awali kwa watu wanaoonyesha dalili na dalili za TB. Zana nyingine za uchunguzi zinaweza kujumuisha hadubini ya smear ya sputum na X-rays ya kifua.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Translation:**

Ikiwa mtoa huduma ya afya ana shaka kwamba mtezi ana ugonjwa wa TB, watapeleka mtezi kwa mtihani. Katika kesi ya shaka ya ugonjwa wa TB wa kifikra, wataomba mtezi awape sampuli ya sputum kwa mtihani wa bakteria za TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa kifikra, sampuli za kioo cha mwili na tishu zilizovimwa zinaweza kuchanganuliwa. Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO) linapendekeza uchunguzi wa haraka wa utambuzi wa kiini kama uchunguzi wa kwanza kwa watu wanaonyonywa na ishara na dalili za TB. Zochorisho za utambuzi zingine zinaweza kujumuisha uchunguzi wa sputum smear na picha za nguvu za kifua (chest X-rays).

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Mtoa huduma ya afya"** -  This is a common and respectful way to say "healthcare provider."
*   **"Shaka"** -  This translates to "suspicion" or "doubt," which is a good fit for the context.
*   **"Mtezi"** - This is the standard Swahili word for "patient."
*   **"Ugonjwa wa TB"** -  "TB disease" is directly translated.
*   **"Sampuli ya sputum"** -  "Sputum sample" is translated literally.
*   **"Bakteria za TB"** - "TB bacteria" is translated literally.
*   **"Shirika la Afya ya Dunia (WHO)"** -  The WHO acronym is included in parentheses for clarity.
*   **"Uchunguzi wa haraka wa utambuzi wa kiini"** - This is a more precise translation of "rapid molecular diagnostic tests" than a more literal rendering.
*   **"Ishara na dalili"** -  "Signs and symptoms" is translated accurately.
*   **"Zochorisho za utambuzi zingine"** - "Other diagnostic tools" is translated accurately.
*   **"Picha za nguvu za kifua (chest X-rays)"** -  This is the standard Swahili term for chest X-rays.

**Important Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable version across different Swahili-speaking regions.  If you need a translation specifically tailored to a particular region (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya, Uganda), there might be slight adjustments to vocabulary.

translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable.
translations.reference: Kwa maambukizi ya TB, mtu huambukizwa na bakteria ya TB ambayo haifanyi kazi katika mwili. Maambukizi haya yanaweza kukua na kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wao wa kinga utadhoofika. Watu wenye maambukizi ya TB hawaonyeshi dalili zozote za TB. Ili kutambua maambukizi ya TB, watoa huduma za afya watachunguza wagonjwa walio katika hatari ili kudhibiti TB hai, na wanaweza kutumia kipimo cha ngozi au damu ili kuangalia maambukizi ya TB. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa TB unatibika.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and naturalness:

**Na ugonjwa wa TB, mtu hupata maradhi ya TB bakteria ambayo yana katika mwili bila kushirikiana. Ugonjwa huu unaweza kuendeleza kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wa kinga wa mtu huweka. Watu wenye maradhi ya TB haoni dalili zozote au ishara za TB. Ili kutambua maradhi ya TB, wataalamu wa huduma za afya watafanyia maswali wagonjwa wanaoshukiwa kuwa wana hatari ili kuashiria ikiwa kuna TB inayofanya kazi, na wanaweza kutumia upimaji wa ngozi au uchunguzi wa damu ili kuangalia ikiwa kuna maradhi ya TB.**

**Treatment: Ugonjwa wa TB unaodegewa unafanyika.**

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"TB infection"** translated as "maradhi ya TB" (TB disease/infection).
*   **"TB disease"** translated as "ugonjwa wa TB" (TB disease).
*   **"immune system weakens"** translated as "mfumo wa kinga wa mtu huweka" (the person's immune system weakens).
*   **"healthcare providers"** translated as "wataalamu wa huduma za afya" (healthcare professionals).
*   **"screen at-risk patients"** translated as "watafanyia maswali wagonjwa wanaoshukiwa kuwa wana hatari" (they will ask patients suspected of being at risk).
*   **"rule out active TB"** translated as "kuashiria ikiwa kuna TB inayofanya kazi" (to indicate if there is active TB).
*   **"skin or blood test"** translated as "upimaji wa ngozi au uchunguzi wa damu" (skin test or blood test).
*   **"Treatment"** translated as "Treatment" (kept the same as it's a common term).
*   **"TB disease is curable"** translated as "Ugonjwa wa TB unaodegewa unafanyika" (TB disease is treatable/curable).

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and understandable to a Swahili speaker.  If you have any specific context or target audience for this translation, please let me know, and I can refine it further.

translations.source: It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Inatibiwa kwa kozi ya kawaida ya miezi 6 ya antibiotiki 4. Dawa za kawaida ni pamoja na rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya matukio, bakteria ya TB haijibu dawa za kawaida. Katika kesi hii, mgonjwa ana TB sugu ya dawa. kwa TB sugu ya dawa ni ya mda mrefu na ngumu zaidi.
translations.hypothesis:
Hivyo hutafiki kwa kozi ya miezi sita ya tiba za kawaida zinazofunika tiba za bakteria za kuvimba. Dawa zinazotumika mara nyingi ni rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika kesi zingine, bakteria za kuvimba hazielezeki na tiba za kawaida. Katika kesi hizi, mtezi ana kuvimba la bakteria lisololeo. Kuvimba la bakteria lisololeo ni mrefu zaidi na ngumu zaidi.

**Note:** I've aimed for a natural-sounding Swahili translation.  There might be slightly different ways to phrase certain parts, but this captures the meaning accurately.

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.
translations.reference: Muda wa dawa za TB hutolewa kwa mgonjwa kwa taarifa, usimamizi na usaidizi na mfanyakazi wa afya au mtu wa kujitolea aliyefunzwa. Bila msaada kama huo, ufuasi wa matibabu unaweza kuwa mgumu. Ikiwa matibabu haijakamilika vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa sugu wa dawa na unaweza kuenea. Katika kesi ya maambukizi ya TB (ambapo mgonjwa ameambukizwa na bakteria ya TB lakini sio mgonjwa), matibabu ya kuzuia TB yanaweza kutolewa ili kukomesha kuanza kwa ugonjwa huo. Tiba hii hutumia dawa sawa kwa muda mfupi.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and naturalness:

**Dawa za kutibu huduma za wagonjwa wa kifua kiba na zinatoa taarifa, usaidhi na msaada na mtaalamu wa afya au mtoa huduma mwenye elimu. Bila msaada huu, ufuaji wa tiba unaweza kuwa ngumu.  Ikiwa tiba haikamiliki vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa na ulemivu wa tiba na kuenea. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kifua kiba (ambapo mwanamke ameuambukizwa bakteria wa kifua kiba lakini haujawa na ugonjwa), tiba ya kuzuia kifua kiba inaweza kutolewa ili kuzuia kuanza kwa ugonjwa.  Tiba hii hutumia dawa zimetu zimefichwa kwa muda mfupi.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Huduma za wagonjwa wa kifua kiba:**  "Patients with TB" -  A common and clear way to express this.
*   **Mtaalamu wa afya:** "Health worker" -  A standard term.
*   **Mtoa huduma mwenye elimu:** "Trained volunteer" -  Captures the meaning of someone with training.
*   **Ufuaji wa tiba:** "Treatment adherence" -  A good translation for this concept.
*   **Ulemivu wa tiba:** "Drug resistance" -  This is a widely understood term in Swahili medical contexts.
*   **Kuzuia kuanza kwa ugonjwa:** "Prevent the onset of disease" -  Clear and direct.
*   **Dawa zimetu zimefichwa kwa muda mfupi:** "The same drugs for a shorter time" -  This phrasing is more natural in Swahili.

**Important Note:**  Swahili has regional variations. This translation is generally understandable across East Africa.  If you need a translation specifically for a particular region (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya, Uganda), there might be slightly different preferred phrasing.

translations.source: Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Chaguo za matibabu za hivi majuzi zimefupisha muda wa matibabu hadi mwezi 1 au 3 tu, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 iliyopita.
translations.hypothesis: Hakika, chaguo za matibabu za hivi karana zimefanyia hivyo kwamba muda wa matibabu umeghara hadi miezi moja au mitatu, ikilinganishwa na miezi sita katika zamani.

translations.source: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
translations.reference: Mionzi ya ultraviolet Muhtasari: Mionzi ya Ultraviolet (UV) hufunika masafa ya urefu wa 100-400 nm, ambayo ni masafa ya juu na urefu wa chini wa mawimbi kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Mionzi ya UV hutoka kwa jua, lakini pia inaweza kuundwa na vyanzo vya bandia vinavyotumiwa katika sekta, biashara na burudani. Kanda ya UV inashughulikia safu ya urefu wa 100-400 nm na imegawanywa katika bendi tatu: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). Mwangaza wa jua unapopita kwenye angahewa, UVC zote na takriban 90% ya mionzi ya UVB humezwa na ozoni, mvuke wa maji, oksijeni na dioksidi kaboni.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Maelezo ya Nyuzi za Ultraviolet**

Nyuzi za ultraviolet (UV) zinaenea katika masafa ya mawimbi kutoka 100 hadi 400 nm, ambayo ni frekensi ya juu zaidi na mawimbi ya chini zaidi kuliko taa ya mwonekano. Nyuzi za UV huundwa kwa asili kutoka kwa jua, lakini zinaweza pia kuundwa na vyanzo vya bandia vinavyotumika katika viwanda, biashara na burudani.  Eneo la UV linaenea katika masafa ya mawimbi kutoka 100 hadi 400 nm na imegawanywa katika aina tatu: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm) na UVC (100-280 nm).  Pale mwanga wa jua unapitia anga, nyuzi zote za UVC na karibu% 90 za nyuzi za UVB hushamlizwa na ozone, mvua, oksijeni na dioksidi ya kaboni.

**Notes on the translation:**

*   I've used "Maelezo ya Nyuzi za Ultraviolet" for "Ultraviolet radiation Overview" – a common and understandable way to introduce the topic.
*   I've maintained the technical terms (UVA, UVB, UVC) as they are frequently used even in Swahili scientific contexts.
*   I've aimed for a natural and flowing Swahili style.

Would you like me to translate any other documents, or perhaps refine this translation further based on a specific context or audience?

translations.source: UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.reference: Mionzi ya UVA huathiriwi kidogo na angahewa. Kwa hiyo, mionzi ya UV inayofikia uso wa Dunia kwa kiasi kikubwa inajumuisha UVA na sehemu ndogo ya UVB. Kiasi cha mionzi ya UV kutoka kwenye jua ambayo hupiga uso wa Dunia inategemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na urefu wa jua angani, latitudo, kifuniko cha mawingu, urefu, unene wa safu ya ozoni na kutafakari kwa ardhi. Kupungua kwa tabaka la ozoni kwa sababu ya uchafuzi unaotengenezwa na binadamu huongeza kiwango cha UVA na UVB kinachofika kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini na maisha ya mimea.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Radia ya UVA inasababishwa kidogo na anga. Kwa hiyo, nyuzi za UVA zinazoshuka kwenye uso wa Dunia zimechanganyikiwa kwa kiasi kubwa, na kwa hisia ndogo za UVB.  Kiasi cha nyuzi za UVA kutoka kwa jua ambazo zinatumbukia kwenye uso wa Dunia hutegemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na kimo cha jua angani, istara, usafiri wa mawingu, kimo cha juu, unene wa safu ya ozone, na miongozo ya ardhi.  Ugonjwa wa ozone unaosababishwa na mchumo wa mazingira unaongeza kiwango cha UVA na UVB zinazoshuka kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, viumbe haibaolojia, viumbe wa baharini na maisha ya mimea.**

**Explanation of some choices:**

*   **Radia ya UVA:**  Direct translation of "UVA radiation."
*   **inasababishwa kidogo na anga:** "is less affected by the atmosphere" -  This phrasing captures the nuance of being *less* affected.
*   **nyuzi za UVA zinazoshuka:** "UVA radiation reaching" -  "Nyuzi" is a good word for rays or beams.
*   **zimechanganyikiwa kwa kiasi kubwa:** "largely composed" -  "Zimechanganyikiwa" means "are mixed" or "are composed of."
*   **istara:**  This is the Swahili word for latitude.
*   **usafiri wa mawingu:** "cloud cover" -  Literally "travel of clouds."
*   **kimo cha juu:** "altitude" - "height of high"
*   **unene wa safu ya ozone:** "thickness of the ozone layer" -  "Unene" means thickness, "safu" means layer.
*   **miongozo ya ardhi:** "ground reflection" - "Reflections of the earth"
*   **mchumo wa mazingira:** "human-created pollution" -  "Mchumo" means pollution, "mazingira" means environment.
*   **kuathiri:** "impact" -  A common and suitable word for impact in this context.

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and natural-sounding in Swahili.  If you have any specific areas you'd like me to refine or clarify, please let me know!

translations.source: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck.
translations.reference: Kwa wanadamu, kuongezeka kwa mfiduo wa UV kunaweza kusababisha saratani ya ngozi, mtoto wa jicho na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Kinga: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya visa vya saratani ya ngozi katika miongo kadhaa iliyopita kunahusiana sana na kuongezeka kwa mwanga wa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje na vyanzo bandia vya mionzi ya UV kama vile taa za jua na vitanda vya ngozi. Mfiduo kupita kiasi pia ndio sababu kuu ya athari mbaya kwa macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua tahadhari chache rahisi kunaweza kupunguza sana hatari ya hali hizi za afya: Weka kikomo wakati wa jua la mchana kutoka 10 asubuhi hadi 4 p.m., hasa siku ambazo index ya UV iko juu. Wakati miale ya UV ni mikali zaidi na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kutafuta kivuli na kuvaa mavazi ya kujikinga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kofia yenye ukingo ambayo hutoa ulinzi wa jua kwa kichwa chako, uso na shingo.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow:

**Katika binadamu, pua kubwa ya UV inaweza kusababisha kansa za ngozi, cataracts na ugonjwa wa mfumo mkuu wa kinga. translations.source: Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: Tumia kinga ya jua yenye wigo mpana wa angalau SPF 15 - bora zaidi - na upake tena kila saa 2. Epuka taa za jua na vyumba vya ngozi, kwani vinajulikana kuharibu ngozi na macho. Mionzi ya UV ya watoto inapaswa kupunguzwa kwa uangalifu kwa sababu wako katika hali ya ukuaji na kwa hivyo huathirika zaidi na matishio ya mazingira kuliko watu wazima. Kazi nyingi muhimu kama vile mfumo wa kinga haujatengenezwa kikamilifu wakati wa kuzaliwa, na mazingira yasiyo salama yanaweza kuingilia ukuaji wao wa kawaida. Majibu ya WHO: WHO inafanya kazi na Nchi Wanachama na washirika ili kuongeza uelewa wa umma juu ya athari za mionzi ya urujuanimno kupita kiasi.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Tumia mwinyi unaokoa kutoka kwa taa ya jua na lesedi za angalau SPF 15 – bora zaidi ni SPF ya juu zaidi – na ufunge upya kila mara baada ya masaa mawili.  Usitumie taa za jua au vyumba vya kupiga taa, kwani zinajulikana kuharibu ngozi na macho.  Macho ya watoto yanapaswa kuzingatiwa sana ili kupunguzwa, kwa sababu wanakua kwa kasi na hivyo niambukizwa zaidi na hatari za mazingira kuliko watu wazima.  Hela muhimu nyingi, kama mfumo wa kinga, hajasimamishwa kabisa wakati wa kuzaliwa, na mazingira hatari yanaweza kuathiri maendeleo yao ya kawaida. Jibu la WHO: WHO inafanya kazi na nchi za mshirika na washirikiano ili kuongeza uelewa wa umma wa athari za uingizwa kwa taa ya ultraviolet.**

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Mwinyi unaokoa kutoka kwa taa ya jua"** -  This translates to "Broad-spectrum sunscreen" – I've used a more descriptive phrase to convey the meaning.
*   **"Lesedi za angalau SPF 15 – bora zaidi ni SPF ya juu zaidi"** - This directly translates "SPF of at least 15 – ideally higher."
*   **"Ufunge upya kila mara baada ya masaa mawili"** - "Reapply every 2 hours."
*   **"Taa za jua au vyumba vya kupiga taa"** - "Sunlamps and tanning parlours."
*   **"Macho ya watoto yanapaswa kuzingatiwa sana ili kupunguzwa"** - "Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited."
*   **"Wanakua kwa kasi na hivyo niambukizwa zaidi na hatari za mazingira kuliko watu wazima"** - "They are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults."
*   **"Hela muhimu nyingi, kama mfumo wa kinga, hajasimamishwa kabisa wakati wa kuzaliwa"** - "Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth."
*   **"mazingira hatari yanaweza kuathiri maendeleo yao ya kawaida"** - "unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development."
*   **"Jibu la WHO"** - "WHO Response"

translations.source: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.reference: Mpango wa INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Mpango wa Mazingira wa Umoja wa Mataifa, Shirika la Hali ya Hewa Duniani, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utafiti wa Saratani na Tume ya Kimataifa ya Ulinzi wa Mionzi isiyo ya Ion. Inakuza na kutathmini utafiti juu ya athari za kiafya za mionzi ya UV, na kukuza jibu linalofaa kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo na usambazaji wa habari. Malengo ya mpango huo ikiwa ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa kiutendaji na mzuri kuhusu athari za kiafya na kimazingira zitokanazo na mionzi ya jua, kuhimiza nchi kuchukua hatua ili kupunguza hatari za kiafya zitokanazo na UV na kutoa mwongozo kwa mamlaka za kitaifa kuhusu mipango ya uhamasishaji wa jua. Ili kufikia mwisho huu, mpango huo unashirikiana na wataalam na mashirika maalum kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kuhesabu hatari za afya kutokana na mionzi ya UV, kuendeleza utabiri wa kuaminika wa matokeo ya afya na mazingira ya mabadiliko ya mfiduo wa UV na kupungua kwa ozoni ya stratospheric, na kuendeleza njia za vitendo za ufuatiliaji mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya zinazotokana na UV kwa wakati. Mpango huo hutoa zana na miongozo inayohusiana na fahirisi ya UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, vitanda vya jua bandia, utalii na afya ya kazini.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Kitabu cha Mpango wa INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Shirikisho la Kuuza Mazingira, Shirikisho la Ushawishi wa Sayansi ya Hawa, Taasisi Maarufu ya Uchunguzi wa Kansa na Kamati Maarufu ya Ulinzi wa Nyuklia Isiyo na Nguvu.**  Mpango huu unashawishi na kutathmini utafiti kuhusu madhara ya kiafya ya nyuzi za UV, na huunda majibu sahihi kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo na usambazaji wa taarifa. Malengo ya mpango ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa vitendo na thabiti kuhusu athari za afya na mazingira za nyuzi za UV, ili kukuza nchi kuchukua hatua kupunguza hatari za kiafya zinazotokana na nyuzi za UV, na kutoa ushauri kwa mamluki kuhusu programu za uelewa wa jua. Kwa hili, mpango huwashiriki wataalam na mitaalam wa ofisi ili kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, huagiza na kuhesabu hatari za kiafya kutoka kwa nyuzi za UV, huunda utatuzi wa kuaminika wa matokeo ya kiafya na mazingira ya mabadiliko katika nyuzi za UV pamoja na ukucha wa ozone layer, na huunda njia za vitendo za kutanguliza mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya za nyuzi za UV kwa muda. Mpango hutoa zana na miongozo kuhusiana na index ya UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, sunbeds ya bandia, utalii na afya ya wafany kazi.

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

translations.source: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there.
translations.reference: Chanjo ya afya kwa wote Muhtasari: Huduma ya afya kwa wote (UHC) ina maana kwamba watu wote wanapata huduma kamili za ubora wa afya wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapozihitaji, bila matatizo ya kifedha. Inashughulikia mwendelezo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kutoka kwa uhamasishaji wa afya hadi kinga, matibabu, urekebishaji na utunzaji wa uponyaji. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo thabiti ya afya, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa ambayo imejikita katika jumuiya zinazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na ya gharama nafuu ya kufika hapo.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow:

**Maarufu kwa Afya ya Kibila: Maarufu kwa Afya ya Kibila (MAKAB) ni matumaini ya kila mtu kupata huduma za afya zote bora zinazohitaji, wakati wowote na popote wanahitaji, bila kukabiliwa na changamoto za kifedha. Inashughulikia mtozo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kuanzia uenezi wa afya hadi chanjo, matibabu, ahueni na huduma za kumtunza wagonjwa walio na ugonjwa. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo ya afya yenye nguvu, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa, yenye asili katika jamii zinazofanya kazi. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (HAMS) ndiyo njia bora zaidi na yenye gharama nafuu ya kufikia huko.**

**Explanation of some key choices:**

*   **Maarufu kwa Afya ya Kibila (MAKAB):** I've translated "Universal health coverage" as "Maarufu kwa Afya ya Kibila" which is a common and understandable way to express the concept in Swahili.  I've included the acronym (MAKAB) in parentheses for future reference.
*   **Mtozo kamili:**  This translates to "full continuum" and is a standard way to express this concept in Swahili.
*   **Uenezi wa afya:**  This translates to "health promotion."
*   **Chanjo:** This translates to "vaccination" (a key part of prevention).
*   **Huduma ya afya ya msingi (HAMS):**  This is the standard Swahili term for "Primary Health Care."

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and easily understandable for a Swahili speaker.  If you have any specific context or target audience for this translation, please let me know, and I can refine it further.

translations.source: Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.reference: Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua nini cha kushughulikia kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu na rasilimali zilizopo. Kuwekeza katika PHC huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji hayo yote yanatambuliwa, yanapewa kipaumbele na kushughulikiwa kwa njia jumuishi; kwamba kuna nguvu kazi ya afya na utunzaji imara na yenye vifaa; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinachangia katika kukabiliana na mambo ya kimazingira na kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujiandaa kwa ajili ya, kukabiliana na hali ya dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele cha kimkakati cha WHO, kukiwa na lengo la watu bilioni 1 zaidi kunufaika na huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2025. tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza nafasi muhimu wakati wa dharura.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia Umoja wa Afya wa Kiawakili (UAHK) na kuamua kutoa huduma kwa kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu wake na vyanzo vinavyopo. Uinvesti katika Huduma ya Afya ya Kibwana (HAK) huweka tahadhari kuhakikisha kuwa mahitaji yote yamezindika, yameelezewa na yamefanywa katika njia iliyoimarishwa; kwamba kuna wafanyakazi wa afya na huduma wenye nguvu na waliofanyizwa vizuri; na kwamba vito vyote vya jamii vinachangia kukabili mabadiliko ya hali ya angani na mabadiliko ya kiuchumi ambayo yanaathiri afya na ustahili, ikiwa ni pamoja na kupanga, kujibu na kupulizwa baada ya dharau.**

**Jibu la WHO:** Kimaudhui wa UAHK, WHO inapendekeza kurekebisha mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya Huduma ya Afya ya Kibwana (HAK). Kufikia UAHK ni kipaumelo cha WHO, na lengo la kufaidisha watu mabilioni moja zaidi kupitia UAHK kufikia mwaka wa 2025. Katika nchi zenye mifumo ya afya dhaifu, tunaleta msaada wa kiufundi wa kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na huduma za utoaji wa huduma ili kupunguza mapungufu muhimu katika dharau.

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **Umoja wa Afya wa Kiawakili (UAHK):**  This is the standard Swahili translation for Universal Health Coverage.
*   **Huduma ya Afya ya Kibwana (HAK):** This is the standard Swahili translation for Primary Health Care.
*   **Vito vyote vya jamii:**  This translates "all sectors of society" in a way that feels natural in Swahili.
*   **Mabadiliko ya hali ya angani na mabadiliko ya kiuchumi:** This captures the meaning of "environmental and socio-economic factors."
*   **Dharau:** This is the common Swahili word for "emergency" or "disaster."
*   **Jibu la WHO:**  This translates "WHO response" as "WHO's answer/response."

translations.source: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously.
translations.reference: Katika mipangilio thabiti zaidi ya mfumo wa afya, tunasukuma athari za afya ya umma kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera ya mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji kazi. Kazi hii inaungwa mkono na mwongozo na makubaliano ya kawaida; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya SDGs Kazi ya WHO inawiana na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) lengo la 3.8, ambalo linalenga kufikia bima ya afya kwa wote, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya na gharama nafuu za dawa muhimu na chanjo za bei nafuu kwa wote. Ulimwenguni, maendeleo kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika utoaji wa huduma yamekwama huku idadi ya watu wanaokabiliwa na matumizi mabaya ya afya nje ya mfukoni ikiongezeka kila mara.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Katika mazingira yenye nguvu zaidi ya mifumo ya afya, tunaleta athari za afya jamii kwa usambazaji wa huduma za afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera kwa mifumo ya kesho na usaidizi wa kimkakati kuboresha utendaji. Kazi hii inasaidiwa na miongozo na makubaliano ya kanuni; data, utafiti na ubunifu; na uongozi katika maslahi ya mzozo, masuala ya kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, mashirika ya kikoloto, fedha. Malengo ya 2030 ya SDGs. Shughuli za WHO zinaambatana na Malengo ya Maendeleo ya Kudumu (SDG) iliyosasishwa 3.8, ambayo inalenga kufikia usambazaji kamili wa huduma za afya, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za fedha, upatana na huduma bora za afya, upatana na tiba salama, yenye ubora na nafuu kwa wote. Kwa ujumla, maendeleo kuelekea usambazaji kamili wa huduma za afya ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika usambazaji wa huduma yamezuiwa huku asilimia ya watu wanaozungukia gharama za afya za nje za kikomiseri zinazidi kuongezeka kila mara.**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **"In more robust health system settings"**: Translated as "Katika mazingira yenye nguvu zaidi ya mifumo ya afya" (In environments with more strength of health systems).
*   **"drive public health impact"**: Translated as "tunaleta athari za afya jamii" (we bring community health impacts).
*   **"policy dialogue"**: Translated as "mazungumzo ya sera" (policy discussions).
*   **"strategic support"**: Translated as "usasaidiwa na miongozo na makubaliano ya kanuni" (supported by normative guidance and agreements).
*   **"normative guidance and agreements"**: Translated as "miongozo na makubaliano ya kanuni" (normative guidance and agreements).
*   **"data, research and innovation"**: Translated as "data, utafiti na ubunifu" (data, research and innovation).
*   **"diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights"**: Translated as "maslahi ya mzozo, masuala ya kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu" (interests of diplomacy, gender issues, health equity and human rights).
*   **"multisectoral action"**: Translated as "mashirika ya kikoloto" (multisectoral partnerships).
*   **"finance"**: Translated as "fedha" (finance).
*   **"2030 Agenda for SDGs"**: Translated as "Malengo ya 2030 ya SDGs" (2030 SDGs Goals).
*   **"Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8"**: Translated as "Malengo ya Maendeleo ya Kudumu (SDG) iliyosasishwa 3.8" (Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8).
*   **"achieving universal health coverage"**: Translated as "kufikia usambazaji kamili wa huduma za afya" (reaching complete health coverage).
*   **"financial risk protection"**: Translated as "ulinzi wa hatari za fedha" (financial risk protection).
*   **"catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending"**: Translated as "asilimia ya watu wanaozungukia gharama za afya za nje za kikomiseri zinazidi kuongezeka kila mara" (the percentage of people around catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases every time).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:** Swahili, like any language, has nuances.  The best translation depends on the specific context.
*   **Regional Variations:** Swahili has regional dialects. This translation aims for a fairly standard form.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, a native Swahili speaker should review this translation for accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.

translations.source: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset.
translations.reference: Afya ya mjini Muhtasari: Ukuaji wa miji ni mojawapo ya mitindo inayoongoza duniani ya karne ya 21 ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya watu duniani wanaishi mijini - idadi ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% ifikapo 2050. Kwa vile ukuaji mkubwa wa miji katika siku zijazo utafanyika katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukuaji wa miji na mwelekeo mwingine mkuu wa maendeleo ya miji kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, sio kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia labda ndio mali muhimu zaidi ya jiji.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow:

**Muhtasari wa Afya ya Mji:**

Ukimwamo wa mijini ni mojawapo ya mabadiliko makubwa ya dunia katika karne ya 21, na una athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya asilimia 55 za wakazi wa dunia wanaishi katika miji – idadi hii inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi asilimia 68 kufikia mwaka 2050. Kwa kuwa karibu kila ongezeko la mijini litarithibika katika nchi zinazopatikana na vyanzo vya kupungua, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukimwamo wa mijini na mabadiliko mengine ya maendeleo ya mijini kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kushawishi afya. Hii ni muhimu, hasa kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia ni mali muhimu zaidi ya mji.

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **Muhtasari wa Afya ya Mji:**  "Urban health Overview" is translated as "Muhtasari wa Afya ya Mji" which means "Urban Health Summary" or "Urban Health Overview".
*   **Ukimwamo wa mijini:**  This is a common and natural way to express "urbanization" in Swahili.
*   **asilimia:**  The percentage symbol (%) is used directly.
*   **inatarajiwa:**  This conveys the sense of "is expected to".
*   **nchi zinazopatikana na vyanzo vya kupungua:** This translates "developing countries" in a way that is commonly used and understood in Swahili.
*   **kushawishi:**  This is a good translation for "promote" in this context.
*   **mali muhimu zaidi:**  This translates "most important asset" effectively.

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and reads naturally in Swahili.  If you have any specific nuances you'd like me to consider, or if you'd like me to adjust the translation for a particular audience, please let me know!

translations.source: However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, wengi kati ya watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi mijini wanakabiliana na ukosefu wa makazi na usafiri, usafi wa mazingira na usimamizi duni wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao unakiuka miongozo ya WHO. Aina zingine za uchafuzi wa mazingira, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kinachojulikana kama visiwa vya joto mijini, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kutembea, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi hai huchanganyika zaidi na kufanya miji kuwa kitovu cha janga la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na vichochezi vya mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa. Hazina ya WHO kwa Afya ya Mjini Athari: Takriban 40% ya ukuaji wa miji uko katika makazi duni ambayo hayana maji salama na vyoo, na 91% ya watu katika maeneo ya mijini wanapumua hewa chafu. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, ukuaji wa miji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi uma, na kusababisha mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya, vilivyochakatwa.

translations.source: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea;
translations.reference: Wakazi wa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuathiriwa na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao wa nishati ya mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na kupasha joto. Miji huchukua zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati ya ulimwengu na hutoa 60% ya gesi chafuzi, na zile za ndani zinaweza kukumbwa na halijoto ya 3–5º C juu kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yanayozunguka kwa sababu ya upana wake mkubwa wa saruji na nafasi finyu za kijani kibichi. Haya yote yanahatarisha afya ya wakazi wa mijini. Sababu 10 kuu za vifo zinahusiana kwa karibu na ukuaji wa miji wa haraka na usio na mpango, na muundo mbaya wa mijini na mipango. Miji inakabiliwa na mzigo wa kiafya mara tatu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU/UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, nimonia, dengi na kuhara;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for accuracy and natural flow:

**Wawekezaji wa miji pia ni hatari kubwa za athari za mabadiliko ya ikolojia kwa sababu ya wasambaa wao wa kuzalisha nishati kutoka kwa mafuta na gesi kwa ajili ya usafiri, kupikia na joto. Miji inaongoza katika matumizi ya nishati duniani, akili kwa zaidi ya nusu mara tatu, na huzaa gesi za nipelekezo za 60%, na zile zilizoko ndani ya jangwa zinaweza kupata ongezeko la joto la digrii 3-5 kuliko maeneo ya maalumu ya maalumu kwa sababu ya anga zake kubwa za simu na nafasi ndogo za kijani kibuyu. Hii yote inawacha afya ya wasafiri wa miji katika hatari. Wahitaji wengi wa kwanza 10 wa vyanzo vya vifo vina uhusiano mkubwa na ukuaji wa haraka na ushirikiano wa miji, na muundo na mipango mbaya ya miji. Miji zinakabiliwa na mzigo mkuu wa afya wa magonjwa ya viumbe kama vile ugonjwa wa ukorema, kuvimbi, pneumia, malaria na ugonjwa wa kutuliza.**

**Explanation of some choices and considerations:**

*   **"Urban dwellers"**: Translated as "wawekezaji wa miji" (residents of cities).
*   **"Fossil fuels"**: Translated as "mafutha na gesi" (oil and gas).
*   **"Greenhouse gases"**: Translated as "gesi za nipelekezo" (greenhouse gases).
*   **"Concrete"**: Translated as "simu" (concrete).
*   **"Open green spaces"**: Translated as "nafasi za kijani kibuyu" (green spaces).
*   **"Rapid and unplanned urbanization"**: Translated as "ukuaji wa haraka na ushirikiano wa miji" (rapid and unplanned urban development).
*   **"Poor urban design and planning"**: Translated as "muundo na mipango mbaya ya miji" (bad urban design and planning).
*   **"Triple health burden"**: Translated as "mzigo mkuu wa afya" (major health burden).
*   **"Infectious diseases"**: Translated as "magonjwa ya viumbe" (infectious diseases).
*   **Specific diseases**:  I've used common Swahili terms for the diseases listed.

**Note:**  Swahili has many regional variations. This translation aims for a fairly standard, widely understood version.  If you have a specific region in mind (e.g., Tanzania, Kenya), the phrasing might be slightly adjusted.

translations.source: noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.reference: magonjwa yasiyo ya kuambukiza kama vile ugonjwa wa moyo, kiharusi, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha ya barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zao, miji inaweza kuunda fursa kwa afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za hali ya hewa. Sera madhubuti za mijini lazima zitangulize afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuendeleza maisha bora ya mijini, kujenga nguvu kazi yenye tija, kuunda jamii zenye uthabiti na uchangamfu, kuwezesha uhamaji, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi ya watu walio hatarini. Mtazamo wa WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini unazingatia ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi wa mazingira, na viashirio vingine vya mazingira; mipango ya miji yenye afya; mazingira bora na yasiyo na moshi; uhamaji salama na kiafya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo ya chakula na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa na vekta.na kujiandaa kwa miji kwa dharura.

translations.source: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.reference: Kufanya kazi katika sekta zote na kuhakikisha uwiano wa sera katika maeneo mbalimbali ni muhimu katika kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo yanahakikisha kwamba masuala ya afya na usawa yanaunganishwa katika mchakato wa kupanga, uwekezaji na maamuzi ya sera katika ngazi ya ndani. Ili kusaidia Nchi Wanachama kushughulikia vipaumbele vilivyo hapo juu, WHO inafanya kazi ili kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kusaidia watunga sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wanaposhughulikia hatari za kiafya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo juu ya kile kinachofanya kazi, na inasaidia ufuatiliaji wa viashiria muhimu vinavyohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuhimiza mabadilishano ya jiji hadi jiji na kusaidia kuandaa mifumo ya kitaasisi na sera ya utawala bora wa miji kwa afya na ustawi katika miji.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and natural flow:

**Ushirikiano katika sekta tofauti na kuhakikisha muungano wa sera katika masuala mbalicha ni muhimu sana ili kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo huongeza uhakika wa masuala ya afya na usawa katika mipango yote, malalamizi na michakato ya uamuzi katika viwango vya mitaa. Ili kusaidia Nchi Mwakati kujitanguliza masuala haya, WHO inaendelea kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili muwashauri waziria watafiti wa afya wajue jinsi ya kushughulikia hatari za afya. Inatoa zana na mbinu za kusaidia, na pia inasaidia kufuatilia vigezo muhimu vya afya. WHO inataruniana na kushirikiana katika kukuza mahakika kati ya miji na miji, na pia inasaidia kuunda muundo wa taasisi na sera kwa utawala mzuri wa miji kwa afya na ustawi wa wananchi katika miji.**

translations.source: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent.
translations.reference: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unajumuisha aina zote za ukatili dhidi ya watu walio chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Kwa watoto wachanga na watoto wadogo, unyanyasaji unahusisha hasa unyanyasaji wa watoto (yaani unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kingono na kihisia na kutelekezwa) mikononi mwa wazazi na watu wengine wenye mamlaka. Wavulana na wasichana wako katika hatari sawa ya unyanyasaji wa kimwili na kihisia na kutelekezwa, na wasichana wako katika hatari kubwa ya unyanyasaji wa kijinsia. Watoto wanapobalehe, unyanyasaji wa rika na ukatili wa marafiki wa karibu, pamoja na unyanyasaji wa watoto, huenea sana.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a Swahili translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy:

**Ukiukaji wa Haki za Mtoto: Muhtasari**

Ukiukaji wa haki za watoto unahusisha kila aina ya ukiukaji wa haki za watu wenye umri wa miaka 18 na chini. Kwa watoto wadogo na wavulana, ukiukaji wa haki mara nyingi huzunguka uvunjaji wa haki za watoto (yani, uvuguzi mwili, kiafya na kihemoro, pamoja na ukandamizaji na kuwacha) na huja kwa mikono ya wazazi na viongozi wengine. Wanaume na wasichana wana hatari sawa ya uvuguzi mwili na kihemoro, na wasichana wana hatari kubwa ya uvuguzi kiafya. Wakitoka kuwa wazazi, unyanyasaji wa mfikiaji na unyanyasaji wa mpenzi mpendwa, pamoja na uvunjaji wa haki za watoto, huenea sana.

---

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **Ukiukaji wa Haki za Mtoto:**  "Violence against children" is translated as "Ukiukaji wa haki za mtoto" (violation of children's rights).
*   **Muhtasari:** "Overview" is translated as "Muhtasari" (summary).
*   **Uvuguzi:**  I've consistently used "uvuguzi" for "abuse" as it's a common and well-understood term in Swahili.
*   **Wazazi na viongozi wengine:**  "Parents and other authority figures" is translated as "wazazi na viongozi wengine" (parents and other leaders).
*   **Mfikiaji:** "Peer" is translated as "mfikiaji" (those of the same age).
*   **Mpenzi mpendwa:** "Intimate partner" is translated as "mpenzi mpendwa" (loved partner).

I've aimed for a translation that is both accurate and natural-sounding in Swahili.  If you have any specific nuances you'd like me to consider, or if you'd like me to adjust the translation for a particular context, please let me know!

translations.source: Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.reference: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unaweza kuzuiwa. Kuzuia na kukabiliana na unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kunahitaji jitihada za kushughulikia hatari na ulinzi katika viwango vyote vinne vya hatari (mtu binafsi, uhusiano, jumuiya, jamii). Azimio la Mei 2016 la Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha mpango wa kwanza wa utekelezaji wa WHO wa Kimataifa wa kuimarisha jukumu la mfumo wa afya ndani ya mwitikio wa kitaifa wa sekta mbalimbali kushughulikia unyanyasaji wa watu binafsi, hasa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana, na dhidi ya watoto. A Kulingana na mpango huu, WHO kwa kushirikiana na Nchi Wanachama na washirika wengine, imejitolea: Kufuatilia ukubwa wa kimataifa na sifa za ukatili dhidi ya watoto na kuunga mkono juhudi za nchi kuweka kumbukumbu na kupima ukatili huo. Kudumisha mfumo wa taarifa za kielektroniki unaotoa muhtasari wa data ya kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, sababu za hatari na matokeo ya unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto, na ushahidi wa kuzuilika kwake.
translations.hypothesis:

**Ukiukaji wa haki za watoto unaweza kuzuizwa. Kuzuia na kujibu ukiukaji wa haki za watoto unahitaji juhudi zirefushwe kwa makusudi ili zizingatia mambo ya hatari na mambo ya kulinda katika viwango vitano vya uhusiano katika jamii (kijana, mahali ya familia, jamii, jamhuri). Katika azma ya Mei 2016 ya Kamati ya Afya ya Dunia, iliyopitishwa, ilithibitisha mpango wa tanzu wa Dunia wa WHO wa kuongeza jukumu la mfumo wa huduma za afya katika majibu ya kitaifa ya jamii ili kukabiliana na ukiukaji wa uhusiano, hasa dhidi ya wasichana na watoto. Kulingana na mpango huu, WHO katika ushirikiano na Nchi za Mshirika na washiriki wengine, imeshaahidi: Kufuatilia ukubwa na sifa za ukiukaji wa haki za watoto duniani na kusaidia juhudi za nchi za kuchunguza na kupima ukiukaji huu. Kuendeleza mfumo wa taarifa wa mtandaoni ambao unahesabu data ya kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, mambo ya hatari na matokeo ya ukiukaji wa haki za watoto, na ushahidi wa uwezo wa kuzuia ukiukaji huu.**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

translations.source: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children,
translations.reference: Kutengeneza na kusambaza hati za mwongozo wa kiufundi, kanuni na viwango vya kuzuia na kukabiliana na ukatili dhidi ya watoto kulingana na ushahidi. Kuchapisha mara kwa mara ripoti za hali ya kimataifa kuhusu juhudi za nchi kushughulikia unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kupitia sera za kitaifa na mipango ya utekelezaji, sheria, programu za kuzuia na huduma za kukabiliana. Nchi zinazounga mkono na washirika katika kutekeleza mikakati ya uzuiaji na majibu yenye msingi wa ushahidi, kama ile iliyojumuishwa katika INSPIRE: Mikakati saba ya kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto. Kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kupunguza na kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto duniani kote, kupitia mipango kama vile Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto,

translations.source: Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.
translations.reference: kwa pamoja kwa Wasichana na Muungano wa Kuzuia Ghasia. Unyanyasaji wa watoto Unyanyasaji wa watoto ni unyanyasaji na utelekezwaji unaotokea kwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Inajumuisha aina zote za unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kihisia, unyanyasaji wa kijinsia, kutelekezwa, uzembe na unyonyaji wa kibiashara au mwingine, unaosababisha madhara halisi au yanayoweza kutokea kwa afya, maisha, ukuaji au utu wa mtoto katika muktadha wa uhusiano wa mtoto wajibu, uaminifu au madaraka. Mfiduo wa unyanyasaji wa rafiki wa karibu wakati mwingine hujumuishwa kama unyanyasaji wa watoto.

translations.source: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners:
translations.reference: Unyanyasaji wa watoto una madhara makubwa sana ya papo hapo na ya muda mrefu. Zaidi ya kifo, jeraha la kimwili na ulemavu, vurugu inaweza kusababisha dhiki ambayo huharibu ukuaji wa ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii nayo inahusishwa na kucheleweshwa kwa maendeleo ya utambuzi, utendaji duni wa shule na kuacha shule, matatizo ya afya ya akili, majaribio ya kujiua, kuongezeka kwa tabia zinazohatarisha afya, urekebishaji na maandalizi ya unyanyasaji. Habari njema ni kwamba unyanyasaji wa watoto unaweza kuzuiwa kupitia hatua zinazosaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na maadili yasiyo ya ukatili, kutoa elimu na mafunzo ya stadi za maisha, kuimarisha mapato ya familia na usalama wa kiuchumi, kutoa mwitikio wa hali ya juu na huduma za usaidizi, kuunda na kudumisha mazingira salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kutekeleza sheria dhidi ya unyanyasaji wa watoto. WHO, kwa kushirikiana na washirika kadhaa:

Zaidi ya vifo, majeraha ya mwili na ulemekezo, uvunjaji wa uhakika unaweza kusababisha mvutano ambao unazidi kuathiri maendeleo ya ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii kwa upande wake inahusiana na ulemekezo wa akili mchache, utendaji mbaya shuleni na wasiwa wakatoka shule, matatizo ya afya ya akili, majaribu ya suicide, tabia hatari za afya, upya wa ukiukaji na uvivu wa uvunjaji wa uhakika. Habari njema ni kwamba uharibifu wa watoto unaweza kuzuishwa kwa mashughuli za kusaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na thamani za siasa za kupinga, kutoa elimu na kozi za ujuzi wa maisha, kuimarisha uchumi wa familia na usalama wa kiuchumi, kutoa huduma za majibu na usaidizi bora, kuunda na kudumisha mazinga wa salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kuthibitisha sheria dhidi ya uharibifu wa watoto. WHO, kwa ushirikiano na washirikishi wengi:**

**Notes on the translation and choices made:**

*   **Uharibifu wa watoto:**  "Child maltreatment" -  A direct and commonly used translation.

translations.source: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development;
translations.reference: Inajumuisha aina mbalimbali za vitendo kutoka kwa uonevu na mapigano ya kimwili, hadi unyanyasaji mkali zaidi wa kingono na kimwili hadi mauaji. Vurugu za vijana huongeza kwa kiasi kikubwa gharama za afya, ustawi na haki ya jinai; hupunguza tija; na kwa ujumla hudhoofisha muundo wa jamii. Zaidi ya vifo, majeraha na madhara ya kisaikolojia, unyanyasaji wa vijana unaweza kusababisha kuongezeka kwa tabia hatari za kiafya kama vile kuvuta sigara, matumizi mabaya ya dawa za kulevya, ngono isiyo salama na vurugu zaidi. Haya nayo yanahusishwa na magonjwa sugu ya kupumua, saratani, magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, kisukari, mimba za utotoni na magonjwa ya zinaa. Mikakati madhubuti ya kuzuia na kukabiliana nayo ni pamoja na ile inayokuza uzazi na maendeleo ya utotoni;

translations.source: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life.
translations.reference: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake - hasa unyanyasaji wa mpenzi wa karibu na unyanyasaji wa kijinsia - ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma na kiafya na ukiukaji wa haki za binadamu za wanawake. Umejikita katika na kuendeleza ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia.Ulimwenguni 1 kati ya wanawake 3 hupata unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kingono maishani mwao, hasa na mwenzi wa karibu. Hiki ni ukumbusho kamili wa ukubwa wa usawa wa kijinsia na ubaguzi dhidi ya wanawake. Umoja wa Mataifa unafafanua unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama "kitendo chochote cha unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ambacho husababisha, au kinaweza kusababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono, au kiakili au mateso kwa wanawake, ikiwa ni pamoja na vitisho vya vitendo kama hivyo, kulazimishwa au kunyimwa uhuru kiholela, iwe unafanyika hadharani au katika maisha ya kibinafsi.

translations.source: Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.reference: Vurugu za mpenzi wa karibu hurejelea tabia ya mshirika wa karibu au mpenzi wa zamani ambayo husababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono au kisaikolojia, ikiwa ni pamoja na unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kulazimishwa kingono, unyanyasaji wa kisaikolojia na kudhibiti tabia. Unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ni "tendo lolote la ngono, jaribio la kupata tendo la ngono, au kitendo kingine kinachoelekezwa dhidi ya ujinsia wa mtu kwa kulazimishwa, na mtu yeyote bila kujali uhusiano wake na mwathiriwa, katika mazingira yoyote. Inajumuisha ubakaji, unaofafanuliwa kama kupenya kwa kulazimishwa au vinginevyo kwa lazima kwa uke au mkundu kwa kutumia uume, sehemu nyingine ya mwili au kitu." Athari: Unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake ni tatizo la kimataifa la uwiano wa janga. Inasababisha uharibifu, madhara kwa maisha ya wanawake na ya watoto wao.

translations.source: It also hurts the economic and social health of their families, communities, and countries. While some women are more at risk than others, violence can happen to any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion or economic status. Gender inequality including harmful gender norms are key drivers of violence against women. Violence can negatively affect women’s physical, mental, sexual, and reproductive health. It is associated with increased risk of injuries, depression, anxiety disorders, unplanned pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV and many other health problems.

Ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia ikiwa ni pamoja na kanuni hatari za kijinsia ni vichochezi muhimu vya unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake. Ukatili unaweza kuathiri vibaya afya ya wanawake kimwili, kiakili, kingono na uzazi. Inahusishwa na hatari kubwa ya majeraha, huzuni, matatizo ya wasiwasi, mimba zisizopangwa, magonjwa ya zinaa, VVU na matatizo mengine mengi ya afya.
translations.hypothesis: Hii pia huruhusu afya ya kiuchumi na kijamii ya familia zao, jamii zao na nchi zao. Ingawa baadhi ya wasichana wana hatari zaidi kuliko wengine, uvumilivu unaweza kutokea kwa yoyote yule mwanamke, katika nchi yoyote - bila kujali utamaduni, dini au hali ya kiuchumi. Utawala wa kijinsia, pamoja na mifano ya kiafya ya kijinsia, ni mambo muhimu ya kusababisha uvumilivu dhidi ya wasichana. Uvumilivu unaweza kuhusiana na athari hasi kwa afya ya kimwili, akili, kimwili na uzazi wa wasichana. Inaashiria hatari iliyoongezeka ya majeraha, huzuni, magonjwa ya hizi na ugonjwa wa hofu, uugaji wa mimba usichozuiwa, magonjwa yaambukizo ya ngono, ugonjwa wa HIV na masuala mengine mengi ya afya.

translations.source: Children who grow up in families where there is violence may suffer a range of behavioural and emotional disturbances. These can lead to mental and development problems and also be associated with perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO response: WHO plays a key role in bringing attention to and responding to violenceagainst women as a public health and gender equality issue through: Research and evidence-building to highlight the magnitude of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and to identify effective interventions for prevention and response. Developing guidelines and tools, setting norms and standards for an effective health response to violence against women.
translations.reference: Watoto wanaolelewa katika familia ambazo kuna jeuri wanaweza kukumbwa na misukosuko mbalimbali ya kitabia na kihisia. Haya yanaweza kusababisha matatizo ya kiakili na ukuaji na pia kuhusishwa na kufanya au kukumbana na ukatili baadaye maishani. Jibu la WHO: WHO ina jukumu muhimu katika kuleta umakini na kujibu unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama suala la afya ya umma na usawa wa kijinsia kupitia: Utafiti na kujenga ushahidi ili kuangazia ukubwa wa unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake, sababu zake za hatari na matokeo yake, na kutambua afua madhubuti za kuzuia na kukabiliana nazo. Kutengeneza miongozo na zana, kuweka kanuni na viwango vya mwitikio madhubuti wa kiafya kwa ukatili dhidi ya wanawake.
